title: North Indian Dal Tadka

subs.name: masoor dal (red lentils) for a quicker-cooking version that does not need a pressure cooker

ingredients.name: ghee
visual_checks: split: 1 tbsp for cooking, 1 tbsp for tadka
ingredients.notes: split: 1 tbsp for cooking, 1 tbsp for tadka

subs.name: oil for a vegan version; the tadka will still be excellent, just less rich
skip the garlic in the tadka and double the asafoetida for a no-garlic version

steps.text: Rinse the toor dal until the water runs clear. Pressure cook with 3 cups water, turmeric, green chilli and a pinch of salt for 4–5 whistles until completely soft. Mash or whisk the dal until smooth; it should have the consistency of a pourable porridge.

Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a saucepan over medium flame. Fry the onion until golden, about 5 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and cook until they break down and oil separates, about 5 minutes.

Pour the cooked dal into the onion-tomato base. Add red chilli powder, garam masala and enough hot water to reach your preferred consistency — dal tadka is usually thinner than dal makhani. Simmer for 8 minutes, stirring often.

Make the tadka just before serving: heat the remaining 1 tbsp ghee in a small pan over high flame until it begins to smoke lightly. Add cumin seeds and let them sputter. Add the sliced garlic and fry 20–30 seconds until golden at the edges. Add dried red chillies and asafoetida for 10 seconds more.

Pour the entire blazing tadka immediately over the simmering dal. Do not stir right away — let the sizzle and smoke infuse for 30 seconds, then stir gently. Taste for salt and garnish with coriander. Serve with rice or roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: toor-dal → masoor dal (red lentils) for a quicker-cooking version that does not need a pressure cooker; ghee → oil for a vegan version; the tadka will still be excellent, just less rich; garlic → skip the garlic in the tadka and double the asafoetida for a no-garlic version
